01.$description: Use as the background of accent-01 colored tags.

02.$description: Use as the background of accent-02 colored tags.

03.$description: Use as the background of accent-03 colored tags.

04.$description: Use as the background of accent-04 colored tags.

05.$description: Use as the background of accent-05 colored tags.

06.$description: Use as the background of accent-06 colored tags.

07.$description: Use as the background of accent-07 colored tags.

08.$description: Use as the background of accent-08 colored tags.

09.$description: Use as the background of accent-09 colored tags.

01.$description: Use as the text color inside accent-01 colored tags. Pair with tag/bg/accent-01.

02.$description: Use as the text color inside accent-02 colored tags. Pair with tag/bg/accent-02.

03.$description: Use as the text color inside accent-03 colored tags. Pair with tag/bg/accent-03.

04.$description: Use as the text color inside accent-04 colored tags. Pair with tag/bg/accent-04.

05.$description: Use as the text color inside accent-05 colored tags. Pair with tag/bg/accent-05.

06.$description: Use as the text color inside accent-06 colored tags. Pair with tag/bg/accent-06.

07.$description: Use as the text color inside accent-07 colored tags. Pair with tag/bg/accent-07.

08.$description: Use as the text color inside accent-08 colored tags. Pair with tag/bg/accent-08.

09.$description: Use as the text color inside accent-09 colored tags. Pair with tag/bg/accent-09.

canvas.$description: Use for the main application background — the lowest visual layer beneath all other surfaces.

inverse.$description: Use as the background for components displayed in inverse (dark) mode, such as navigation bars, notifications, popover tips, and step card badges.

white.$description: Use as the elevated container surface for cards, modals, drawers, dropdowns, tables, and other floating or panel-style components.

brand.$description: Use as the background fill for brand-colored UI elements such as progress bars and brand tags.

tooltip.$description: Use exclusively for tooltip container backgrounds.

subtle.$description: Use for subtle background fills in loading states and secondary containers such as avatars, cards, progress bar tracks, slider rails, and table cells.

muted.$description: Use for low-contrast background fills that de-emphasize sections, such as file upload zones, table header cells, and upload areas.

sunken.$description: Use for recessed UI elements that appear lower in the visual hierarchy, such as scroll-bar tracks.

dark.$description: Use for the strongest border emphasis in scenarios that require maximum contrast, such as file upload drag-and-drop zones.

default.$description: Use for standard border outlines on interactive and structural components such as buttons, cards, drawers, dropdowns, tables, and sliders.

strong.$description: Use for high-emphasis borders that require stronger visual weight, such as file upload areas, loaders, and progress bars.

active.$description: Use to indicate the active, selected, or focused state of interactive elements such as sliders, tabs, file upload zones, and progress indicators.

inverse.$description: Use for borders on components displayed against dark or colored backgrounds, such as badges, icon buttons, and dividers.

primary.$description: Use for the highest-priority text and icons across the UI. Apply to headings, labels, and primary content in accordions, breadcrumbs, modals, tables, and search.

secondary.$description: Use for supporting text and icons that accompany primary content, such as subtitles, placeholders, and helper icons in dropdowns, inputs, cards, and tables.

brand.$description: Use for brand-colored icons and text in components that reinforce brand identity, such as active states in pagination, segmented controls, and tabs.

inverse.$description: Use for text and icons that appear on dark or inverted surfaces, such as badges, buttons on dark backgrounds, navigation, notifications, and tooltips.

tertiary.$description: Use for the lowest-emphasis text and icons, such as muted labels and helper text in sliders and table toolbar content. Also known as: gray

bg-default.$description: Use as the default background of the primary button.

bg-hover-click-on.$description: Use as the background of the primary button in both hover and pressed states.

bg-default.$description: Use as the default background of the destructive primary button.

bg-hover.$description: Use as the background of the destructive primary button in the hover state.

content-on-surface.$description: Use as the text and icon color inside the destructive primary button.

bg-click-on.$description: Use as the background of the destructive primary button in the pressed state.

content-on-surface.$description: Use as the text and icon color inside the primary button.

bg-default.$description: Use as the default background of the secondary button.

bg-hover.$description: Use as the background of the secondary button in the hover state.

border.$description: Use as the default border of the secondary button.

bg-default.$description: Use as the default background of the destructive secondary button.

bg-hover.$description: Use as the background of the destructive secondary button in the hover state.

border.$description: Use as the border of the destructive secondary button.

content-on-surface.$description: Use as the text and icon color inside the destructive secondary button.

bg-click-on.$description: Use as the background of the destructive secondary button in the pressed state.

content-on-surface.$description: Use as the text and icon color inside the secondary button.

bg-click-on.$description: Use as the background of the secondary button in the pressed state.

border-hover.$description: Use as the border of the secondary button in the hover state.

content-on-surface-default.$description: Use as the default text and icon color inside the text button.

content-on-surface-click-on.$description: Use as the text and icon color inside the text button in the pressed state.

bg-click-on.$description: Use as the background of the text button in the pressed state.

bg-hover.$description: Use as the background of the text button in the hover state.

content-on-surface-hover.$description: Use as the text and icon color inside the text button in the hover state.

focus.$description: Use as the focus ring color applied to all button variants in the focused state.

bg.$description: Use as the background of any disabled button regardless of variant.

content-on-action.$description: Use as the text and icon color of any disabled button regardless of variant.

bg-surface.$description: Use as the surface background of the destructive ghost button.

bg-hover.$description: Use as the background of the destructive ghost button in the hover state.

border.$description: Use as the border of the destructive ghost button.

content-on-surface.$description: Use as the text and icon color inside the destructive ghost button.

content-on-surface-default.$description: Use as the default text and icon color inside the ghost button.

bg-hover.$description: Use as the background of the ghost button in the hover state.

bg-click-on.$description: Use as the background of the ghost button in the pressed state.

content-on-surface-hover-focused-click-o.$description: Use as the text color of the link button in hover, focused, and pressed states.

content-on-surface-default.$description: Use as the default text color of the link button.

bg-default.$description: Use as the default background of the tertiary button.

bg-click-on.$description: Use as the background of the tertiary button in the pressed state.

border.$description: Use as the border of the tertiary button.

content-on-surface.$description: Use as the default text and icon color inside the tertiary button.

content-on-surface-hover-click-on.$description: Use as the text and icon color inside the tertiary button in hover and pressed states.

bg-default.$description: Use as the default background of the icon-only tertiary button.

bg-click-on.$description: Use as the background of the icon-only tertiary button in the pressed state.

content-on-surface.$description: Use as the icon color inside the icon-only tertiary button.

bg-hover.$description: Use as the background of the icon-only tertiary button in the hover state.
Use as the background of the notification button in the hover state.

content-on-surface.$description: Use as the text and icon color inside the notification button.

content-on-surface-disabled.$description: Use as the text and icon color inside the notification button when disabled.

border.$description: Use as the border of the notification button.

subtle.$description: Use as a light-tinted background for low-emphasis critical feedback areas.

elevated.$description: Use as the prominent background for high-emphasis critical feedback components.

muted.$description: Use as a mid-tinted background for medium-emphasis critical feedback areas.

on-surface.$description: Use as the text and icon color for critical error messages and alerts.

border.$description: Use as the border color for critical feedback components such as error alerts and banners.

subtle.$description: Use as a light-tinted background for low-emphasis success feedback areas.

elevated.$description: Use as the prominent background for high-emphasis success feedback components.

muted.$description: Use as a mid-tinted background for medium-emphasis success feedback areas.

border.$description: Use as the border color for success feedback components.

on-surface.$description: Use as the text and icon color for success messages and confirmation states.

subtle.$description: Use as a light-tinted background for low-emphasis warning feedback areas.

elevated.$description: Use as the prominent background for high-emphasis warning feedback components.

muted.$description: Use as a mid-tinted background for medium-emphasis warning feedback areas.

on-surface.$description: Use as the text and icon color for warning messages.

border.$description: Use as the border color for warning feedback components.

on-surface-elevated.$description: Use as the text and icon color for warning messages displayed on elevated/prominent surfaces.

on-surface.$description: Use as the text and icon color for informational messages.

subtle.$description: Use as a light-tinted background for low-emphasis informational feedback areas.

elevated.$description: Use as the prominent background for high-emphasis informational feedback components.

muted.$description: Use as a mid-tinted background for medium-emphasis informational feedback areas.

border.$description: Use as the border color for informational feedback components.

surface.$description: Use as the surface background for neutral/default feedback components.

on-surface.$description: Use as the text and icon color for neutral/default feedback messages.

border.$description: Use as the border color for neutral/default feedback components.

default.$description: Use as the default background color of primary action elements such as filled buttons and CTAs.

hover.$description: Use as the background of primary action elements in the hover state.

click-on.$description: Use as the background of primary action elements in the pressed state.

inverse.$description: Use as the background of primary action elements displayed on dark or inverted surfaces.

active.$description: Use as the default background color of primary action elements such as filled buttons and CTAs.

default.$description: Use as the default border of primary action elements.

focus.$description: Use as the focus ring color of primary action elements in the focused state.

hover+click-on.$description: Use as the border of primary action elements in the hover state.

inverse-active.$description: Use as the text and icon color on inverted primary action surfaces.

default.$description: Use as the default text and icon color on primary action surfaces.

hover.$description: Use as the text and icon color on primary action surfaces in the hover state.

subtle.$description: Use as the subdued text and icon color on primary action surfaces.

active.$description: Use as the default text and icon color on primary action surfaces.

bg-hover.$description: Use as the background of secondary action elements in the hover state.

bg-default.$description: Use as the default background of secondary action elements.

bg-click-on.$description: Use as the background of secondary action elements in the pressed state.

on-surface.$description: Use as the text and icon color on secondary action surfaces.

border.$description: Use as the border color of secondary action elements.

secondary.$description: Use as the background of disabled secondary action elements.

tertiary.$description: Use as the background of disabled tertiary action elements.

primary.$description: Use as the background of disabled primary action elements.

content-on-surface.$description: Use as the text and icon color on any disabled action element.

border.$description: Use as the border color of any disabled action element.
Use as the border color for destructive/danger action elements.

content-on-surface.$description: Use as the text and icon color for destructive/danger action elements.

subtle.$description: Use for standard, low-emphasis borders and dividers across most UI components.

bold.$description: Use for high-emphasis strokes that require maximum visual prominence, such as progress bar edges or selected state indicators.

strong.$description: Use for medium-emphasis strokes that need slightly more visual weight, such as focused or active input borders.

screensm.$description: Use to define the small breakpoint for layout adjustments on smaller desktop screens.

screenxl.$description: Use to define the extra-large breakpoint for wide desktop screens.

screenxs.$description: Use to define the extra-small breakpoint for responsive layout adjustments on small screens.

screenbase.$description: Use as the base breakpoint for the standard desktop layout.

screenxlmax.$description: Use to define the maximum breakpoint for ultra-wide screens and large displays.

12.$description: Use for moderate spacing in compact layouts or between related form elements.

16.$description: Use for default component spacing in standard layouts, such as padding in inputs and buttons.

20.$description: Use for comfortable spacing between components or within containers that need more breathing room.

24.$description: Use for generous spacing between component groups or within larger containers.

32.$description: Use for wide spacing between major layout sections or section-level containers.

48.$description: Use for the largest spacing between page-level content blocks or hero sections.

56.$description: Use for the largest spacing between page-level content blocks or hero sections.

02.$description: Use for the tightest spacing between closely grouped elements, applicable as margin, padding, or gap.

04.$description: Use for very compact spacing in tightly packed components such as inline tags or icon groups.

08.$description: Use for small spacing between related UI elements, such as icon-to-label gaps in buttons and inputs.

12.$description: Use for pronounced corner rounding on modals, dialogs, and larger surface containers.

24.$description: Use for heavily rounded corners on pill-shaped elements such as tags, badges, and segmented controls.

04.$description: Use for subtle corner rounding on small interactive elements like inputs, dropdowns, and compact cards.

00.$description: Use for fully circular shapes such as avatars, circular icon buttons, and status indicators.

08.$description: Use for moderate corner rounding on cards, panels, and containers.

06.$description: Use for light corner rounding on standard buttons, form fields, and table rows.
